spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used: (1) Lexical — renamed several columns to semantically equivalent names. 'Project ID' → 'Budget Ref ID' (the instruction refers to it as 'budget ID in column H', so renaming tests whether the model hardcodes the header name), 'Budget Type' → 'Revenue Category' (instruction says 'revenue types' in column L), 'Project includes tech?' → 'Includes Technology?' (answer column), and several uninvolved columns renamed to break hardcoded header matching. Importantly, I did NOT rename 'technology' or 'Budget ID' (col J) since 'technology' appears as a general English word in the instruction and 'Budget ID' in col J is distinct from the 'budget ID' reference in the instruction which points to col H. (2) Positional title row insert — inserts a title row pushing headers from row 1 to row 2 and data from rows 2-11 to rows 3-12, which breaks hardcoded row references. The answer position shifts from Q2:Q11 to Q3:Q12. (3) Distractor column — 'Tech Flag Override' is plausibly similar to the answer column 'Includes Technology?' but clearly different, testing whether the model writes to the wrong column.
